phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Mathematics, chemistry, and engineering fundamentals with environmental orientation.

milestones.label: Explore engineering pathways
milestones.description: Introduction to engineering disciplines with focus on natural resources and environmental engineering career pathways.

milestones.label: Master calculus foundations
milestones.description: Derivatives and integrals with applications to environmental flow modeling and resource analysis.

milestones.label: Introduction to biological engineering
milestones.description: Survey of biological engineering with emphasis on natural resource applications.

milestones.label: Complete general chemistry
milestones.description: Chemistry foundations for soil science, water chemistry, and environmental analysis.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Advanced math, physics, and biology build the scientific foundation for environmental engineering.

milestones.label: Complete calculus II
milestones.description: Advanced integration and differential equations for hydrological modeling.

milestones.label: Master engineering physics
milestones.description: Mechanics, thermodynamics, and fluid properties for environmental systems.

milestones.label: Study ecology & biology
milestones.description: Ecology, organism biology, and ecosystem science foundations for ecological engineering.

milestones.label: Master CAD & technical drawing
milestones.description: Technical drawing and CAD for watershed maps, site plans, and engineering designs.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Specialized coursework in soil & water engineering, water resources, and ecological design.

milestones.label: Master soil & water conservation engineering
milestones.description: Engineering design for soil erosion control, watershed management, and conservation practices. Students analyze USDA NRCS design standards for agricultural best management practices.

milestones.label: Study water resources engineering
milestones.description: Hydrology, hydraulics, and water resources planning. Students design stormwater management systems and analyze water quality using engineering models.

milestones.label: Design ecological engineering systems
milestones.description: Integration of ecological principles with engineering design for constructed wetlands, green infrastructure, and ecosystem restoration. Students develop designs for real sites in the NC Piedmont region.

milestones.label: Complete transport phenomena
milestones.description: Heat and mass transfer applied to environmental systems, groundwater flow, and contaminant transport modeling.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Industry-sponsored capstone, FE exam prep, and professional placement complete ABET requirements.

milestones.label: Begin capstone design project
milestones.description: First semester of industry-sponsored capstone. Teams address natural resources engineering challenges like watershed restoration or green infrastructure design.

milestones.label: Complete capstone & present to industry
milestones.description: Final capstone delivery with complete engineering designs, cost analysis, and environmental impact assessment. Professional presentation to industry sponsors.

milestones.label: Prepare for FE exam
milestones.description: Structured FE exam preparation covering environmental engineering, hydrology, and biological engineering topics.

milestones.label: Complete engineering internship
milestones.description: Supervised placement at environmental engineering firm, USDA NRCS, EPA, or water utility. Students apply ABET competencies in professional settings.
